newsSinceJun6: No material new fundamentals since June 6. Today's +2.74% (to 46,500) is continued digestion of pre-window catalysts: the June 4-5 institutional briefing on the new medium-term plan (FY3/31 operating profit target ~Y250B vs Y95B FY2026 guidance, well above consensus per Goldman's constructive note from late May), Daiwa/Morgan Stanley Buy maintains (June 4), and the upcoming 10-for-1 stock split (effective ~July 1) that will broaden retail access. Sell-side is now 7 Buy / 0 Sell with avg PT ~Y63,878 - the name is no longer under-covered.

sources.label: CNBC quote/news 5801.T (analyst actions, June 4-5 management policy briefing, stock split schedule)
Knockouttrader on X - Goldman constructive on Furukawa, mid-term plan FY3/31 OP Y250B well above consensus
Globe and Mail/TipRanks - Furukawa FY2026 results, dividend hike, 10-for-1 stock split
Lightera/Furukawa - Mie ultra-high-fiber-count cable plant, 13824-count mass production for AI data centers
BigGo Finance - FY2025 full-year call summary, data-center demand drove OP to Y63.9B

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday already moved this down for the rerating; nothing genuinely new since June 6 and +2.74% is drift, not news. The 'less crowded than Fujikura' leg keeps eroding (7 Buy/0 Sell coverage, Goldman constructive, retail-friendly 10-for-1 split coming July 1), so hold with mild negative drift rather than a second consecutive down move.
verdicts.bull: The mid-term plan targets ~Y250B FY3/31 OP vs Y95B FY2026 guidance - a real, above-consensus fundamental step-change in fiber/optics earnings power, and avg sell-side PT (~Y63,878) still sits ~37% above spot.
verdicts.neutral: At +341% YTD and $21.4B cap with unanimous sell-side endorsement, Furukawa is a recognized AI-fiber play; residual hidden alpha on a latency-node lens is thin even if estimates keep rising.
verdicts.bear: Crowding plus a 5-year OP target as the valuation anchor leaves the stock exposed to any data-center capex digestion or fiber pricing slip; the July 1 split invites momentum/retail flow that can unwind fast.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 10-for-1 stock split (effective ~July 1) plus Q1 FY2026 results validating the Y250B mid-term plan trajectory.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding: any hyperscaler fiber order pause or mid-term-plan credibility wobble compresses a now consensus-owned multiple.

verdicts.rankRationale: The down move for the YTD rerating was taken yesterday; since June 6 there is no new contract, capacity, or guidance datapoint - only continued digestion of the above-consensus mid-term plan. On this alpha-scored optical/networking bucket the exposure is real but fully flagged, so hold with slight drift down.
verdicts.bull: Ultra-high-count fiber (13,824-count Mie plant at full-scale production) and the Y250B FY3/31 OP target give concrete scale-out bandwidth leverage that consensus numbers may still under-model.
verdicts.neutral: Execution is on track and estimates are rising, but at +341% YTD the market has already paid for much of the optics ramp; risk/reward is now roughly symmetric.
verdicts.bear: A 2.6x OP-growth plan is the bull case priced in; fiber/cable is cyclical and any AI data-center capex deceleration or Fujikura/peer capacity race on pricing hits the rerated multiple hardest.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY2026 earnings and new hyperscale fiber-capacity or design-win announcements out of the Lightera/Mie ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical fiber pricing and capex digestion against a valuation that now embeds the full mid-term plan.
